title: 'Being with baboons in Wilderness Heights' - a workshop
description: GEORGE NEWS - A half-day workshop with...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - A half-day workshop with Wynter Worsthorne on the topic of understanding the resident troops in Wilderness Heights and finding methods for peaceful coexistence is being presented on Saturday 8 February from 14:00 to 18:00.


> Worsthorne, South Africa's most respected international animal communicator and teacher in animal communication, will be focusing on working with intuitive interspecies communication (IIC) to assist in creating a more harmonious coexistence between humans and baboons.


The event is an Animaltalk Africa Trust initiative and will focus specifically on Wilderness Heights baboons, but people who are concerned about baboon human relations in other areas are welcome. Bookings are essential to ascertain attendance numbers.

"I am hoping this will be a pilot workshop to roll out in other in other areas around South Africa to help humans to understand their furry cousins a bit better and to learn how to co-exist peacefully," says Worsthorne.

"This is promoting a heart-based method of moving forward, using a combination of the emerging science of IIC alongside academic research."

The event is held at Fairy Knowe Hotel. To confirm attendance, whatsapp Wynter Worsthorne on 078 115 4894.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
